prediction.reasoning:
The price prediction and confidence intervals are derived using the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a common method for modeling stock price movements. The current price of XOM ($147.28) is used as the starting point. For the 20-day forecast, an annualized drift (expected return) of 5% and an annualized volatility of 25% were assumed. The time horizon was converted to years (20/252 trading days). 

- The point estimate represents the expected future price under the assumed drift. 
- The confidence intervals are calculated based on the log-normal distribution of stock prices implied by the GBM model, using the assumed volatility to determine the spread around the expected price. Higher confidence levels naturally result in wider intervals.
